courseGroups.name: Core Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Core Courses: 16 credits

courseGroups.name: Required Course
courseGroups.ruleText: Follow the selection rules shown on the official programme page.

courseGroups.name: Elective Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 14 elective credits, including at least one elective from each of Engineering, Business and Management, and Science. The annual course list is announced separately.

tracks.name: Chinese Language and Literature
tracks.ruleText: Complete four courses (12 credits) in the Concentration, including at least one required course (3 credits). The intake-specific Concentration elective list is not published on the official curriculum page.

tracks.name: Chinese History and Anthropology
tracks.ruleText: Complete four courses (12 credits) in the Concentration, including at least one required course (3 credits). The intake-specific Concentration elective list is not published on the official curriculum page.

courseGroups.name: Foundation Course
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete LANG 5072. An approved exemption requires one additional elective course so that the 24-credit Programme total is maintained.

courseGroups.name: Required Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete two required courses (6 credits) from the official pool. Named HUMA substitutions require approval. These courses remain available without a Concentration; the Concentration qualification separately requires at least one applicable required course.

courses.name: History and Theory of Comparative Literature
Languages of China: Anthropological and Cognitive Dimensions
Traditional Chinese Literary Theory and Criticism
Chinese Culture in Jin Yong's Novels
Fundamentals of Chinese Philosophy
courses.description: This course is designed to guide students to in-depth researches into important issues in Chinese philosophy. The subject matter of the course may vary from one year to another depending on the particular interests of the instructors.

courseGroups.name: Elective Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 15 elective credits from the annual China-related elective list published by the Division of Humanities. Surplus required-course credits may count toward this requirement; the fixed annual elective codes are not published on the official curriculum page.

tracks.name: History and Culture
tracks.ruleText: Optional disciplinary Concentration: complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from the official History and Culture list.

tracks.name: Political Science
tracks.ruleText: Optional disciplinary Concentration: complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from the official Political Science list.

tracks.name: Quantitative Social Science
tracks.ruleText: Optional disciplinary Concentration: complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from the official Quantitative Social Science list.

tracks.name: Sociology
tracks.ruleText: Optional disciplinary Concentration: complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from the official Sociology list.

tracks.name: Academic Research
tracks.ruleText: Complete the existing 24-credit coursework requirement plus 3 credits of Research Methodology, MGCS 6200 (3 credits), and MGCS 6980 with MGCS 6982 (12 credits).

courseGroups.name: Core Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 9 Core credits from the official pool. Surplus Core credits count toward the Elective requirement.

courses.name: Literary and Cultural Studies Humanities Seminar
A Comparative Perspective of Religion in Ancient Civilizations

courseGroups.name: Conditional Academic Writing Course
courseGroups.ruleText: LANG 5070 is required only for students admitted with any IELTS sub-score of 5.5.

courses.name: Critical Writing for Global China Studies

courseGroups.name: Elective Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 12–15 Elective credits, covering at least two official subject areas and including at least two China-related courses. Up to 9 approved substitution credits may apply; surplus Core credits count here.

courses.name: The Economic and Political Development of Modern Japan

courseGroups.ruleText: Selected students may take MGCS 6981 for 1 extra credit; it does not count toward the Programme credit requirement.

courses.name: Research Experience in Global China Studies

courseGroups.name: History and Culture Concentration Requirement
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from courses marked for the History and Culture Concentration. The official page says five disciplinary Concentrations but enumerates only four; no missing fifth Concentration has been inferred.

courseGroups.name: Political Science Concentration Requirement
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from courses marked for the Political Science Concentration. The official page says five disciplinary Concentrations but enumerates only four; no missing fifth Concentration has been inferred.

courseGroups.name: Quantitative Social Science Concentration Requirement
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from courses marked for the Quantitative Social Science Concentration. The official page says five disciplinary Concentrations but enumerates only four; no missing fifth Concentration has been inferred.

courseGroups.name: Sociology Concentration Requirement
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete at least 9 credits (three courses) from courses marked for the Sociology Concentration. The official page says five disciplinary Concentrations but enumerates only four; no missing fifth Concentration has been inferred.

courseGroups.ruleText: With prior approval, Academic Research students may substitute SOSC 5090 for the Core course MGCS 5010.

tracks.name: Psychology
tracks.ruleText: Optional Concentration: complete at least three courses (9 credits) from the official Psychology required-course list.

courseGroups.name: Foundation Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: MA students complete both Foundation Courses for 6 credits.

courseGroups.name: Required Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 12 credits from the term-specific SSMA required-course list covering one or more social science disciplines. The fixed course-code list is announced by the Programme Office each term and is not published on the curriculum page.

courseGroups.name: Elective Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 6 elective credits from eligible School of Humanities and Social Science postgraduate courses; at most one approved 4000-level undergraduate course may count.

courseGroups.name: Psychology Concentration Course Pool
courseGroups.ruleText: Optional Psychology Concentration: complete at least three courses (9 credits) from this official pool. This is a subset of the MA Required Course requirement and needs manual audit review.

courseGroups.name: Required Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: Required Courses: 12 credits

courses.name: Biotech Venture Leadership: Navigating Science and Business

courseGroups.name: Students are required to take at least three ELBT courses from the following list
courseGroups.ruleText: Follow the selection rules shown on the official programme page.

courses.name: Applications of AI in Different Biotech Sectors
Managing Biotech Projects, Teams and Operations
Global Pharmaceutical Policy: Rationales and Stakeholders
Big Data, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Drug Safety
Market Access for Pharmaceutical Products: Trends and Challenges
Principles of Clinical Trials for Regulatory Approval

tracks.name: Infrastructural System Engineering and Management
tracks.ruleText: Optional MSc Concentration: complete at least 12 credits in the selected sub-area. The Programme itself requires breadth across at least three sub-areas.

courseGroups.name: Required Course Pool
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete 24 credits from the official course pool across at least three sub-areas. Approved JEVE and IBTM courses may each contribute up to 6 credits under the published substitution limits.

courses.name: Building Information Modeling and Smart Construction
Construction Technology for Civil Engineering Works
Alternative Dispute Resolution: Law and Practice
Structural Analysis and Design of Tall Buildings
Building Aerodynamics and Wind-Resistant Design of High-Rise Structures
Computer Aided Design Optimization of Tall Buildings
Risk Assessment and Contaminated Land Cleanup
Design and Management of Physico/Chemical Processes of Environmental Engineering
Topics in Civil Infrastructural Engineering and Management
courses.trackRuleText: Counts toward one or two approved sub-areas according to the Special Topics content and Programme Director approval.

courseGroups.name: MSc Project
courseGroups.ruleText: Complete the mandatory 6-credit CIEM 6980 MSc Project. Its approved topic may cover one or two sub-areas.

courses.name: MSc Project
courses.trackRuleText: Counts toward one or two approved sub-areas according to the project topic.

courseGroups.name: Foundation Courses
courseGroups.ruleText: MSc students must complete at least 18 foundation credits, including at least three management-related courses. Official substitution rules remain subject to Programme approval.

courseGroups.ruleText: The pre-approved elective list, including JEVE 6980, is made available at the beginning of every term; the official page does not state a fixed elective-group credit minimum.
